text:
The last product that I tried with vitamin C in it was a daily serum from Avon, and it left my face burning, itchy and red. Not so with the PHL Vitamin C Hydrating Cleanser!
I have been using this cleanser morning and night for about a week now and I like the results! I look more awake in the morning after using this product and the circles under my eyes are not as dark. There is no itching or burning sensation when applying this product. It hydrates the skin; my face feels a little firmer and is not dry and itchy. My son has been using this product as well and it helps to control his acne. In one week the pimples on his forehead reduced to 1/3 of their size and became less red by simply using this cleanser.
My cleaning routine starts by squeezing a quarter sized portion of cleansing lotion into my palm. I use my hands to apply the lotion to my face and neck, leave it on for 30 seconds to 1 minute, then gently rinse most of it off with my hands and warm water using circular scrubbing motions. Finally I pat my face and neck dry with a towel.
The closest product that I have used that is similar to this cleanser is the Cetaphil Gentle Skin Cleanser, but it did not list the antioxidants and vitamin C that the PHL cleanser contains.

I can't live without this stuff. It has spoiled me with crazy-long lashes. When I stop using it for a few months my lashes seem to look so..... normal! (The tragedy)! It seems to be just fine when I limit it to 2 or 3 times a week. I suspect that wearing it on days when my eyes water a lot makes it worse. Leaving it on for more than 24 hours causes pain as well.

text:
My order arrived quickly and in perfect condition, no leaks.I have purchased fractionated coconut oil from a lot of other companies it I feel this is the best. I highly recommend it.
Coconut oil comes in two forms for skin and hair care. One is solid, cold-pressed, extra virgin, the other is a clear liquid. Fractionated oil has a longer shelf life and it is liquid when at room temperature. In this liquid state it is ideal for many applications such as a base for essential oils, or as is for an after shower instant moisturizer.
Lots of companies sell this form of coconut oil but I chose Sky Organics for a good reason.
I have purchased a wide variety of products from Them over this last year each and every one has been delightful. I know I can count on getting the very best organic products from them. Organic products have been through the greatest scrutiny to be able to be called organic. Whether you care if your products are organic or not, no one can deny the quality.
This oil comes with an additional pump top, which makes it easy to use when I am making an aroma therapy blend. The top that comes with it, is great for quick applications, such as in the shower.

text:
I grew up using electric shavers, and until recently had never ever used a multi-blade razor.

I've used this shaver at least 3 times a week for about 2 years. During that time I needed to change shaving heads twice (which seems fairly normal to me).

Things I liked about this razor are that it holds a charge for a reasonably long time, it does a decent job, and it lasted me a fairly long time. I feel like I got my money's worth.

What I didn't like about it was that the blades had a really tough time snagging hairs when it's warm or humid. You definitely need to make sure that your face is very dry when using it, or else you'll find yourself pressing it against your skin to try to pick up a stray hair that it missed.

The other thing I would mention is that it had a habit (especially toward the end of its life) of yanking on hair or two when I shaved with fairly thick stubble. Nothing too awful, but a surprising jolt now and then.

Overall it does a reasonable job in a pinch, and for a long time I never really knew what a good, close shave felt like so I was always content with what I got out of this shaver. For something that you will be using several times a week, I would put down a few extra $$ and get a nicer model.

text:
MWF was one of the first soaps I discovered early on when I returned to DE shaving, and after I figured out how to get it lathered - it’s been a great baseline to use as a comparison point to other vendor’s products. It is listed as unscented, and with the exception of a lanolin scent - it is.
Lather:
MWF is known to be difficult to lather. The trick seems to be dialing in the water amount (usually more than other shave soaps) that it needs to properly lather. However, once you’ve figured out how to dial it in for your specific water - it is easy to repeat your success in subsequent shaves. Hint – a good brush makes a WORLD of difference here… Do I want to take this camping, or use it in the field – No. Too much work to lather and uses too much water (a heavy resource to pack in). At home where I have a sink and running water - it’s fine.
Slickness:
When properly lathered, MWF is one of the slickest soaps I’ve ever used. For several years I have used it as the baseline when evaluating other shave soaps slickness. I don’t know if it’s the lanolin, or the extra amount of water required to get it lathered that makes it so slick, but by goodness it works. The best part is that when properly lathered, the slickness lasts and does not dry out too quickly. I can lather my entire face at the beginning of my shave, and not be rushed to complete it before the lather dissipates.

text:
Well they say “expectations are the source of all unhappiness”, and maybe that’s the problem. I was expecting this soap to be significantly better than the glycerin-based soaps I’ve been using (which cost a third of what this does), and it just... isn’t. It does a good job, and it lasts a long time, but I didn’t notice any improvement in lubricity or lather richness over my previous soaps.
And the scent was inoffensive, but underwhelming.
As for long-lasting, see the attached picture for what the soap looks like after two months of daily use. It (quite literally) barely made a dent in it. So that’s a plus in the “value” department.
One other issue that may or may not be the soap’s fault: the steel travel tin I was using it in had been used for months with other soaps, and no problems. With this soap, it rusted to uselessness in two months. (The inside, around and under the soap, was completely oxidized and flaking off.) That was curious to me, but I don’t have enough data to say it was definitely caused by the soap.
In summary, it’s a decent soap. Kind of expensive, but I don’t mind paying for quality. But somehow, I was expecting something like mythical powers out of a lanolin soap, and they’re just not there.

text:
Ok, I am going to write this review on all five of the products in their organic line. I am a firm believer in not mixing products. Some I gradients do not work well with others, so I feel that using an entire line gives the best results, especially in skin care. First you start with the Super detox charcoal activated deep pore facial cleanser. This is black, due to the charcoal. It has a light floral/witch hazel-y smell to it. It is organic, vegan and cruelty-free. Made with Aloe Vera and organic essential oils, along with being infused with reiki (healing energy). Next is the exfoliating mask. I used the pineapple and papaya one. It comes in a 2.3 oz glass jar. I don't really smell the fruity aspect of it, but I DO smell the Roman chamomile. It says it is meant for sloughing, but this is gelatinous, like pure Aloe Vera gel. I did not notice any e folk taking or tightening properties, but my skin did feel nice and clean after using it. It did not sting as it dried, and it washed off easily with warm water. Again, infused with reiki and natural ingredients.
The next step is the toner. I have the lavender and aloe, as well as the rose crystal. The lavender is balancing and used for hydrating and clarifying. It comes in a 4.5 oz glass spray bottle, has a wonderful floral scent and is also infused with reiki. The rose water toner is made with rose quartz crystals and is good for nourishing, moisturizing and restoring. It smells of wonderful roses. Same size as the lavender.
The final step is the chamomile moisturizer. This smells similar to the mask, but it is a creamy consistency. Loaded with antioxidants, I use a quarter sized amount and pat it onto my toner dampened skin. It comes in the same size jar as the mask.
This set is a bit pricey, however, as the ingredients are all natural and organic, you are going to pay a bit more. Also, this is price comparable to any natural product line in the salons. What I like the most about these products is that they are all natural, GMO free, vegan friendly and cruelty free. My skin feels clean after using these. It does not feel greasy, nor does it feel "scrubbed"...just a natural, gentle clean...WITHOUT harmful toxins going into my body. For anyone who has battled cancer, this is extremely important when deciding what to put in or on your body!
The only reason I dropped it a star was for the simple fact that the mask does not technically exfoliate. It is another cleansing layer, but does not actually slough off any skin. Other than that, I really like this set! Seeing as this set is infused with healing reiki energy as well, It makes it a no brainer in my book!

text:
I've been trying to find some press on nails for a while now. I have long nails/nail beds so finding press on's that actually cover my nails have been a challenge. Took a chance on these and when I got them I was excited because they were pretty, long enough, and seemed to be good quality so I thought they would last a while. Getting them on was simple enough. I had no issues. But once they were on I couldn't function normally (think contacts stuck in my eye) so I had to cut some of the length off. They cut and filed beautifully so that was awesome! But as you can tell from the pictures they do not wear well. Those pictures were taken 36 hours apart. The glitter ones wore better but the grey are looking ruff, especially my pointer fingers. I was happy I would be able to use these nails again (I have enough sticky pads to put them on again after these come off) but I won't be able to now with them looking like this :\

I did put a couple layers of top coat over the glitter nails because they were shedding glitter just a bit so maybe if I had done the same for the gray they would look slightly better? I'm not really sure. I would probably try this brand one more time just to see if maybe this set wasn't the best but I don't have thee highest of hopes

text:
I have used the original Murray's Superior many times over the years...usually when in a pinch or when my preferred pomades were not available. I'm not going to lie...I've never cared for it. The scent has historically smelled like one of your grandmother's candles and the consistency was not unlike, well...glue...and not in a good way. It did not spread easily, comb through easily or come out easily and I often felt like I pulled out more hair the usual using it. It also tended to be rather greasy, even though most heavy pomades offer more of a matte finish.

I'm pleased to say that this Vintage Fragrance formulation of Murray' Superior is quite different. It still holds well, but is creamier and less glue-like. It spreads and combs much more easily through the hair, but doesn't seem any less effective at holding a pompadour hairstyle neatly in place with a matte finish. It also has a pleasant fragrance to it...sort of a floral fragrance, but not at all unmasculine. I'm not too good at describing it, but finding a scent is one of the hardest things for me with pomades. Most pomades either have a good scent that is too strong, no real scent at all or a gimmicky, unmasculine scent (like bubble gum or pineapple). Some of the more new school ones just smell like chemicals. This Murray's formulation smells like something out of an old-school barber shop...something your dad or granddad would have worn on date night to slay the ladies. Murray's hit it out of the park with this one. I'm not sure if this is a limited edition or an ongoing product, so I'm going to buy a few more cans while I can.

As to my ratings...I did 4 out of 5 on value because, even though it's a good price for a can of pomade, it is on the high side for Murrays. Scent = 5 out of 5. Easy to clean...well, it's an oil-based pomade, so not exactly easy to clean, BUT it DOES come out much more easily than traditional Murray's Superior. So that's a plus. Will buy this again. It may just be my current fave. I like the new oil-based Suavacito too, but its scent, while nice, is a bit overbearing.

Try this stuff. Thank me later.

text:
GLO Science Teeth Whitening Pen 35 Aplications, Safe and Efective, No Sensitivity, Easy to Use Dentist Invented Whitening Treatment, Travel-Friendly

Are my teeth whiter? Yes. It's not so white that anyone will see me and be like 'wow your teeth are so white!' but it is whiter and I notice it, which I prefer. I have very sensitive teeth and almost no enamel. The pen is a click pen, and you have click quite a lot to get it started. I thought mine was faulty at first, and the clicker even got stuck half way down but I smacked it and it popped back and I kept clicking. The silicone tip has little bristles and it does a good job of coming off on your teeth - I was worried the gel wouldn't grip onto my teeth and would slide, but it didn't. It does have a cinnamon taste, and it does dry in 30 seconds, it was easy enough to smile for 30 seconds and it was on there. Truly I forgot about it after that (don't eat or drink for 30 min if you can). Are my teeth whiter? I couldn't tell after 1 use, but after two, I also couldn't tell... BUT I took a before and after photo (which I'll save you from because my lips look awful right now) and my teeth are definitely visibly whiter! I'm happy with this, it's easy to put on, doesn't make my teeth sensitive, works for whitening, dries quickly, and you don't even have to spit it out.

text:
This is a great moisturizer for the eye area. It comes in elegant white packaging with gold trim. Inside the box is an amber bottle with pump dispenser to keep the contents safe from light and prevent contamination. The cream itself is lotion like in consistency, pastel orange in color, and distributes easily and is absorbed quickly. It is middle range in on the heaviness scale (gel like being light and emollients being heavy). It does not leave a residue per say, but it does leave your skin feeling velvety and protected which made me feel like it helped my skin retain moisture. It has a fruity fragrance that contains sweet orange notes. It did not sting my dry and sensitive skin. Alcohol, paraben, and silicone free. Made in the USA from an impressive list of botanical (see complete ingredient list below). This is the fourth Valentia product I have tried and I have been happy with all of them. I plan on keeping an eye on this brand.

Complete Ingredient List (for reference):
Deionized Aqua (Water), Organic Aloe Barbadensis (Organic Aloe Vera) Leaf Juice, Cassia Angustifolia Seed Polysaccharide (botanical hyaluronic acid), Methylsulfonylmethane (MSM), Glyceryl Stearate (Plant Derived), Cetearyl Alcohol (Plant Derived), Carthamus Tinctorius (Safflower) Seed Oil, Globularia Cordifolia Callus Culture Extract (plant stem cells), Undaria Pinnatifida Cell Culture Extract, Stearic Acid (Plant Derived), Helianthus Annuus (Sunflower) Seed Oil, Kosher Vegetable Glycerin, lsopropyl Palmitate (Palm Oi), Ubiquinone (CoQ10), Sodium Ascorbyl Phosphate (Vitamin Proprietary Silk Protein Amino Acid Blend, Propriety Essential Oil Biend, Wildcrafted Euphrasia Officinalis (Eyebright), Helichrysum ltalicum (Helichrysum Essential Oi), Wildcrafted Camelia Sinensis (Green Tea), Pentylene Glycol, Phospholipids, Wildcrafted Resina Propoli (Propolis), Organic Olea Europaea (Olive Oi), Melissa Officinalis (Melissa Essential Oil, Proprietary Antiox-Berry Blend, Haematococcus Pluvialis extract (Astaxanthin), Proprietary Wild Flower Blend, Sucrose Stearate, Polyglyceryl 10 Laurate, Lecithin, Polysorbate 20, Potassium Phosphate, Decyl Glucoside (plant derived), Glyceryl Caprylate (Natural Preservative), Sodium Carbomer, Sodium Hydroxide (pH Modifier. Tocopherol (Antioxidant), Ethylhexylglycerin (Preservative), Lonicera Caprifolium (Honeysuckle) Flower Extract, Lonicera Japonica (Honeysuckle) Extract (Natural Preservative).

I purchased this product at a discount in exchange for my honest and unbiased review. If this review was helpful to you, please click on the YES button. This will make it easier for others to find the review so that they may be helped as well and let me know which reviews were helpful.

text:
The only reason I did not give this product five stars is because of the packaging. The cap comes off in two parts for some reason. One part opens the dropper tip and the other opens the entire top of the bottle. When I went to fill the makeup cup of my airbrush I uncapped the wrong part of the Aeroblend bottle and before I realized it I'd literally dumped $24.50 worth of product down the drain... and on the vanity, and on the floor... :(

I used the makeup once before accidentally pouring the rest of the bottle out. But, from my very limited experience, Aeroblend is an amazing product. It evenly covered my very uneven skin and made it look almost flawless. Aeroblend blended perfectly with my actual skin tone (although maybe I just got lucky and chose the right shade on the first try) so it looks completely natural. I'd been using Art of Air makeup and had to do a lot of mixing and experimenting with different shades every time I put it on. Even then I could never get it just right. Aeroblend is very light (not heavy) so it didn't look or feel caked on my skin. It didn't even feel like I was wearing makeup. I definitely recommend this product, just be sure to pay attention when you're opening the bottle.

text:
Worked really well! My hair feels healthy and the color looks great! Only thing is that the color is most vibrant in the sun, and indoors it looks very, very subtle. The instructions are in Japanese but the process is very easy! Watch some youtube videos for guidance if needed. The dye is foam based so it’s super easy to cover your entire head. My hair is slightly thicker and medium length, and one bottle was more than enough for my hair. I left it on for about 45 min, though the box recommends 30. Not sure how long the color will last though!

Edit:
It didn’t last more than three washes. There’s still a definite difference than what i started with, but the red and pinks are completely gone. Still, it was fun and super easy to apply. I feel no damage to my hair and I still like that my color is at least a little different. I didnt use an color shampoo or conditioners but you may be able to maintain the color better if you do. Point for easy application, little to no hair damage, and honestly fun to try.

text:
I have a Luminess Air airbrush system that really does do a nice job of applying foundation with a flawless look. It applies quickly and evenly, but thoroughly cleaning the airbrush EVERY TIME is a MUST in order to avoid makeup drying in the nozzle, effectively preventing it from spraying evenly - or at all - the next time you use it. Cleaning the thing takes a lot more time than the actual spraying on makeup part, and gets annoying fast. Upon first glance I thought this was a great idea for the person who is not ready to commit to a real airbrush, or likes airbrush results but not all the maintenance.

The makeup itself is nice with a good coverage and comes in a number of shades. The one issue which is why I only gave it three stars is that it does not come out in a fine controlled mist, but much heavier like a can of spray paint. Unlike an airbrush where you can control the flow by how much pressure you put on the trigger, this only has one "speed" and it's far more powerful than it needs to be. Then I looked back at the instructions which tell you to spray it on a BRUSH - not your face - and then apply it to your face. This pretty much eradicates the whole appeal of an "airbrush spray foundation" if you have to use a brush to blend it out, no different than if the foundation came out of a bottle. My guess is that the original intention was for it to sprayed directly on the skin like a cordless airbrush, but when that didn't work out quite right, it was marketed instead with instructions to use a brush.

If there was a way of controlling the mist better, therefore being able to use it right from the can to your face, this would be a five star product. As as it is now, though, the "airbrush" bit is more of a gimmick than a useful tool. If Luminess were to tweak the delivery system, this would be a foundation I could see myself using on a regular basis.

I am cautiously optimistic that this product will be helpful, but it's hard to say since I can't scientifically compare the "after" to the "before" . I've been using it for about a month. The shampoo lathers well and I leave it on for a few minutes while I bathe to let it soak in. However, since I can't find an Argan Oil Conditioner that doesn't have to be washed as in shampooed) out I'm not using a conditioner. It seems to me that having to shampoo, then add a conditioner, and have to shampoo it out is more time consuming than necessary. I haven't used any of my non-argan oil conditioners because I'm afraid they might undo whatever the shampoo is supposed to have done. To me, the idea combination is a shampoo and rinse-out conditioner that are meant to compliment each other.

text:
I'm a bit of a shaving geek. I don't have a huge collection, but I do have "a collection". Straight razors, single edge, and even a couple double edge razors.

So my wife picked this up for me last Christmas.

It's not too shabby. Razor has a good feel in the hand, though I wish the handle was a bit shorter. The head is more aggressive than say, a Gillette Tech. Different feel and balance than a Super Speed.

A Feather or Polsilver Super Iridium makes for a decent shave.

The brush is ok. A bit stiff for "badger", but not as stiff as a Boar Brush. Looks good in the set at least, but I'm still getting loose hairs out of it.

The stand: The stand is a bit lacking. A heavier base would have helped, and none of my other brushes fit "quite right" in the stand. It was certainly designed for the included hardware.

text: I was so excited to find this set of 4 neon sequined cheer bows for my daughter. She is a dancer and loves all things bows and sparkly. These bows really deliver on the sparkle, especially in the sun! The colors are fantastic and match so many options for summer kid's clothes this year.  I really loved that the elastic actually matched each bow. Most of the time when I have ordered similar designs of bows, the elastic was actually black. It really took away from the pretty colors of the bow in my opinion. I also like that it was a two layer bow so it made it feel nice and thick. I was concerned though when I saw there was a lot of fraying at the bottom of the bows, where the fabric had been cut. The edges were very jagged and not as nicely made as some other bows we happen to have. The other day my daughter was wearing one of the neon sequin bows, specifically the white color bow if you are wondering, and as she would jump on our trampoline outside the sequins were constantly falling off! I was sadly dissapointed about this. We still love the bows though, just perhaps maybe will treat them with a little more care in the future. So I do think they are fine enough quality for the inexpensive price, however they do not seem to be top notch. I did reveice a discount in exchange for my honest review but the opinions are solely my own.

text:
This color is pretty good for the price. There are a few pros and cons to it that I will explain below, but overall I don't think I will buy it again.

Pro:
Comes with 2 containers which is enough color for any length hair
The ability to adjust the shade you want by adding conditioner to the color
A little bit goes a VERY long way
Price is very reasonable
When it fades, it fades all the way out so it doesn't look like some weird shade of orange when it starts to wash out
You don't need any heat to get the color to stick
It doesn't smell like anything

Cons:
Fades INCREDIBLY fast - I had it in for maybe a week or two before it washed all the way out
You have to use a ton of conditioner to create a light soft pink color - the size of a dime with about 2 cups of conditioner do the trick
It will dye everything it comes in contact with - use thick gloves
If you are diluting the color with conditioner, it will take some time to sit on your hair
If you are diluting the color, you'll never need both bottles - it would take 5 years to use all of it

I think overtone color conditioner is an easier system to use and the color stays longer.

text:
The listing is for 3 sheets of 18 graduated sizes of stickers attached to a central strip, so 6 stickers of any given size. The "54 strips" refers to the quantity of individual stickers, not the quantity of sheets. At $13 USD, that's $4.33 per sheet and $0.24 per sticker. Since most people don't have 9 different sizes of nail beds on every finger, you'll go thru the ones that fit pretty quickly and have to either trim down or throw out the ones that don't fit.

In Sep 2018 I ordered a pack of 40 sheets (720 stickers) from Dashing Diva for $25. That breaks down to roughly $0.62 per strip of 18 and $0.03 per individual sticker. They worked well and I felt happy with the results. Although they've tweaked the product in the last couple years and the stickers are now a thicker material, and 2021 is a different world in terms of supply chain costs, I'm not convinced a ~587% price increase in less than 3 years is justified by slightly thicker material.

Although these strips are effective at protecting your natural nail and easy to use, the value for money is not great in my opinion. Unless the price comes back down I can't see myself ordering these again, and will have to find more cost effective ways to keep my nails healthy.

text:
This comes in an outer product box. It is made in China.

It has a cruelty free logo (PETA), but I don’t believe it’s actually certified by PETA. I hate this but I see it all the time with products I get on Amazon from an unknown brand like this. They have the PETA cruelty free logo. In most cases it’s the old PETA logo that was changed back in 2015. This one actually had the new PETA logo, so I actually thought for once I’d search for the company on the PETA website and they’d actually show up…but they didn’t. I searched for “Pudaier” because that was the brand name on the front of the box, and it didn’t come up. I searched “MAEPEOR,” since the product page said the brand is MAEPEOR. The back of the box said “Cosmetic Production License Guang Dong Makeup,” so I searched that and it didn’t come up. The box also said, “Manufacturer Shantou City Han Ya Cosmetics CO., Ltd.,” so I searched that and it didn’t come up. I know brands can be cruelty free without being certified. I have seen many brands just put “cruelty free” on their packaging and that’s fine. I am more likely to trust that. But, when they put the logo of one of the certification organizations like PETA on their packaging and I search and it’s not there, it makes me not trust the brand at all. If they’re willing to lie a falsely use the PETA logo, then I’m guessing their willing to lie about being cruelty free. I don’t get why brands do this! Do they think people aren’t going to check? I’m not even cruelty free and I still check. So, I’d assume people who are cruelty free would check when it’s a brand they are new to. So it’s just odd to me that brands do it, but enough do it that they must think it works well enough. So, if you’re cruelty free, you’ll probably want to do some more research into this. But, I don’t think this brand is cruelty free.

The ingredients are listed on the box.

It comes in a little tube. The tube has a nice look to it. It’s black. The bottom where you twist seems to be the shade of the foundation, but I’m not for sure on that since I only have the one I got. The bottom part that you twist to twist the stick up moves REALLY easily. Like just barely touching it when you’re handling the stick will move it. With how easily it moves, when I removed the cap I wasn’t surprised the stick had squished up against the top of the lid inside. It was only a little squished, but still a little annoying. I tried to twist it up all the way to show how much product you get, and the whole thing fell out. It seems to have broken off of the inside part. I can put it back in and it stays put when it isn’t twisted up far, but if you turn it over it will fall out pretty easily. So, that stinks. You don’t get very much product either. Stick foundations already go quickly in my opinion, so I don’t see this lasting too long.

I got the shade “02 White.” I loved they had a product photo showing all the shades swatched together. I hate when brands don’t include that. It is already hard enough picking a shade online, it’s even harder when there’s no swatch photos. So I always appreciate it when it’s included!

I am fair and neutral leaning slightly cool.

I did a swatch on my arm showing the color. The swatch is 2 layers. The only other stick foundation I ever found that I liked on my skin is the Lancôme Teint Idole Ultra Wear. So, that’s the only foundation stick I had to use to compare shades. I did a swatch of it next to the swatch of the Bellapierre. The Lancôme one is in the shade “110 Ivoire (C).” As you can see in the swatch, the Lancôme one is definitely more neutral leaning cool. It matches my skin well enough, but I do wish it was lighter. This one is definitely lighter and works well for my skin tone. I feel like it is neutral enough, but does have a slight warm tone to it. It works well enough on my skin tone.

I wasn’t sure what kind of coverage to expect from it. The product page said full, but it also said it was “ultra thin.” It does feel lightweight on the skin. It looks smooth on the skin. It said it is a matte finish, but then describes it as a matte satin finish. I have normal to dry skin, more on the dry side, so I like when a foundation isn’t too matte. This one is definitely matte, but it isn’t too matte where it makes my skin look really dry and cakey. So, I like that.

It says that it can be worn for up to 24 hours and stay on all day with your makeup looking great “even in Arkansas humidity.” I thought that was a weird thing to say 😂 I don’t know about Arkansas humidity, so maybe it isn’t weird?

This has a creamy consistency. It glides onto the skin easily. It blends out easily with a sponge, brush, or fingers. I would say it gives good coverage. I don’t think it’s full right off the bat, but it’s definitely medium to full and can be built up to full.

Overall, I think this is a nice foundation stick. It gives good coverage and can be built up. It is creamy and easy apply and to blend out. It looks nice on my skin. It doesn’t accentuate any dryness at all. You don’t get a lot of product and, like other reviewers, it breaks off very easily. Even though it is easy to apply and blend out and looks nice on the skin, this isn’t something I’d purchase. It’s affordable so I don’t mind not getting a ton of product, but I feel like foundation sticks always go pretty quickly and this one would go fast. Plus, since it broke, there’s only so far I can use it. Right now I’m able to pop it back in and use it because it doesn’t need twisted up far. But, once I get down in product and need to twist it up more, it will just fall off when I do that. So, this isn’t something I’d purchase because of mine coming broken and so many other reviewers coming broken. If you don’t mind that, it is a nice foundation.

I gave it two stars because the concept works, but it's not all that I hoped for...some the manufacturers fault, and some from my own laziness.

text: I wanted to love this stool so much, very well made and beautiful.  As with any saddle stool it takes time to adjust to sitting correctly and getting your sitz bones use to it.  Only two issues the whole portion in the front is a bit large preventing me from getting close to my patient, it gets in the way. I am not afforded that much clearance space in my line of work.  It does force me to sit up straight when I sit in it properly though :) the other issue is the seat is way too wide for me.  The description said 16.5" the seat width is actually close to 20"wide!!!! I am 5'1" and about 108lbs. I don't know if this is a manufacturing error or they are all this way.  That's the only reason I gave it a 3 star, it was very misleading in the description and now I have a stool I can't use and it's not worth me spending shipping charges to return it.....it's already been assembled. text:
There have been a lot of complaints about the vacuum option of this trimmer. Let's be realistic....its not a Hoover, it's a trimmer. It catches MOST of the hairs, which is a lot better than NONE of the hairs. I've used it a few times so far and haven't had the problem others complained about where the setting is changed mid-trim. Just hold the thing below the dial, and not right on the dial. It gave a nice even trim all around. I've had it for a couple of months, used it 3-4 times, and still haven't had to recharge it.

Suggested improvement - A stand would have been great so I could put it in my medicine cabinet and not have it roll around my counter.

Tips for using - I found you're better off with dry hair. When the hair's wet, it's harder for the vacuum to pull it in. I also found that the vacuum works much better on longer hair then on shorter hair. Maybe the shorter hairs are too light and get blown around? Last but not least, watch out for the settings. The dial goes 1-9 so I started with 4, which was way too short. I could have grown that beard from scratch in a couple of weeks. 5 also proved a little short for me. Be sure to start a lot higher than you think you need, and work your way down if needed.

text: I have been using this hair removal device for 2 months now. I used it once every 2 weeks so I've used it 5 times so far. Now, the sessions will be only once a month until all the unwated hair is removed. The manufacturer recommends using it once every 2 weeks for the first 5 sessions and then once a month after that, so using it multiple times a week won't change the outcome. The areas I use this device are my legs, under arms, bikini area and upper lip. The only difference I've noticed so far is the upper lip hair. It is growing significantly less, like almost 90% is gone. The rest of the body hair still grows the same amount, but the hair started to grow softer. I assume the hair is finally becoming weaker. I am half Greek and half Italian, I have olive skin color with dark hair, so it might take over a year for me to see some results. Overall, the device is very easy to use and it is painless, but it gets super hot to the touch after about 5-10 minutes of continues usage. I use the device on my legs first and then take a break until it cools down, and then I continue using it on other parts of the body and stop to cool it down again. I have to admit it is frustrating to stop the session to cool down the device. I don't know if other hair removal devices get this hot. This is my first time using something like this so I can't compare.

No issues whether this soap was used on my face skin vs body skin. My biggest complaint and the reason for only giving three stars is because it CLEAVES. By about the halfway point, the bar cleaves in half. And then each half further breaks into pieces, and then additional pieces rendering the soap useless. This happened with all five of the bars I received. I'm not sure if it is common in naturally made or cold pressed soaps, but I was disappointed. Although each bar is gigantic, only half of the bar is useful and it seems that is what I'm actually paying for. Since bar soap is communal in this household, I could not try a wash cloth bag of sorts to hold the soap together. I suggest if you're a customer who doesn't use bar soap at home communally, then you could try this method if you run into the same issue I did.

text: Package was open when I opened my Amazon box and the contents were all rattling around the box with everything else I bought; if I wanted not-new, open tools I use on my face I could've spent my money on those. However, I choose to purchase this set and was under the impression it was new and unopened. I have to spend time disinfecting them and getting cardboard/packaging microfibers out of the tools now so it doesn't end up in my pores instead of just swiping it with a Lysol wipe and being on my way to fast, easy hair removal; which is annoying and not convenient and costs me more time grooming (which essentially annoys me anyway but I don't like to resemble a Sasquatch so I spend the time and money on this crap begrudgingly anyways), so at least send me new, in-the-package tools because that's what I paid for. Tweezers are ok quality, could have bought at Walmart for a couple of bucks but you can't really own too many pairs of tweezers. Eyebrow shaper is alright, I will stick to my usual brand in the future and could have bought a pack of 3 of my preferred brand for a couple bucks, they were just on sale at Sally's BOGO. The threading tool is kind of nifty. I'm still learning how to use it properly so I'm going to chalk the hair patches on the test spot on my arm as user error. All in all I feel "meh" about this purchase and probably will not buy it again. I feel indifferent about recommending to my girlfriends and if they ask about the threading tool, I'd probably tell them about how it was sent open and just go to a local Sally's.

text:
Assalamu Alaikum. If you are a Muslim or another religion that wears a hijab (headscarf) let me start off by saying that this scarf is NOT for you unless you are purchasing for a child that is 6 or under to wear as hijab.

The length is ok, decent at best but not great. The width is the real issue. It will barely cover a medium to large size cranium such as mine. And forget about the nape of your neck because this will NOT reach that far to offer some covering. You better have a super high tide turtle neck on before trying to sport this as a hijab and leave the house!

As far as colors, they were fairly vibrant and really that was the most positive part of my experience with this scarf (not shawl). The Chiffon material is decent but a little cheaply made compared to other Chiffon hijab scarves I have had. And before someone gets all gins shooting about price and you get what you pay for, well that isn't always true. I get way better quality at heart hijab website and they are based in the UK and they ship all the way to the US where I am temporarily. And that isomething total shipping, not shipping per item so if you do a breakdown of scarves versus shipping and cost per item I found much better quality and price elsewhere.

If you are just wearing this for fashion to tie round your neck or use as an accent in your hair up-dos than this is the perfect size and you will have plenty to work with. But if you are in the same boat as me, a Muslim or someone who dresses and covers for modesty than you will be passing this off to your six year old for dress up.

text:
I honestly hate having to leave a bad review. I used this product for a year and loved it. My skin looked better than it ever had, but then my fourth bottle really failed me.
My first three bottles each lasted me four months. Each seemed a little different in formulation. In the first bottle, the cleanser came out almost like water. Since it was my first bottle, I figured was just how it was (and some of the previous reviews would have me believe so). The second bottle was very foamy, but the smell was different, in a bad way. I kept using it, and kept having great results. The third bottle smelled better, but foamed less.
Then I got the fourth bottle. Smell was normal, foam wasn't great. Then my face began breaking out terribly, and the bottle ran out in 4 weeks!! I reached out to the company, telling them that I was breaking out and that the bottle was gone in four weeks instead of four months. They emailed me back, telling me that their formula hadn't changed, and that maybe I was using more without realizing it. I would assume that a company selling a 4oz bottle of facial cleanser for $30 would be a little concerned instead of accusatory. I assumed that maybe I got the last bottle on a run, so maybe the formula was unbalanced and the bottle wasn't filled properly. I know, for sure, that I did not use quadruple the amount of cleanser each day, and the company assuming that I did is crazy.
To make a long story short, I used to love this stuff, but since the company only offered to send me a 20% off coupon code for my next bottle, instead of a full replacement for a bad bottle, I will not be buying from them again. I really and truly wish it had worked out though.

text:
This amino acid shower gel has a thin consistency, but is not as runny as water.
The gel has a silver-white color, with a mild floral scent.

The gel lathers up decent.
I like how clean I feel after applying it to my body and then rinsing it off. The scent does not linger on my body for long, once I have finished showering.

After a couple of weeks use, on a daily basis, I did not feel my dry skin being less dried, nor moisturized for a long time (albeit, this is a short time testing period).

The product description page mentions that this shower gel is "natural and organic"; yet, I do not see any certification seals on the product bottle.

The product page also says this shower gel is indicated for sensitive skin type; but, it has an astounding 50+ ingredients, including cocamidopropyl betaine.

Expiration date is on the bottom of the bottle. Mine expires 01/05/2024.

Overall, this shower gel does the one thing well that I want a body wash to do - clean my skin.

text: I am very impressed with this Organic Moringa Oil. It has a very slight but engaging smell which is earthy, enticing, and invigorating. That is a good size as a lot of oils come in much smaller bottles and cost just as much. So Moringa oil is filled with antioxidants and phytonutrients along with supposedly reducing wrinkles and sagging fatigued skin. It also boasts anti inflammatory benefits. So here I am to report that the oil, along with being a delight to smell, is refreshing, relaxing, and hydrating to my face and hands. No, my wrinkles have not wondrously disappeared but I can report that my dry skin on my face is being diminished. As for my hands a little bit of the Moringa oil rubbed in both of my hands leaves them feeling so luxuriously hydrated I just want to hold hands with everybody! I'm really enjoying this oil. I think it's a great asset to my unending quest in preserving what small amount of beauty I have left. Plus it makes me feel good and it sure does pamper my skin. I'm enjoying making it a staple of my health and beauty routine.

text:
I bought these I-tip hair extensions to replace some thinning patches I have on my head. I have had hair extensions professionally put in from my hairdresser in the past and she suggested I stick with the I-tip, primarily for the ease of use and convenience. I was hoping it would save some time and money being able to add some volume to my thinning spots. I received them promptly and they looked and felt soft and silky. The ends were quite thin and wispy. I ordered the 18” and felt like I only got about 14” of quality hair. The color selection are very good and accurate. I was able to match it perfectly with my own hair color. I also liked the fact you can color you hair with them in.
With these extensions you have the option of glueing them in or using the beads to clamp them in. I chose the beads. One of the first things I noticed was how thick the tip of the hair piece was with glue. Being that I have thinning hair you can see the bead and tip at times. Note.. If you blow dry your hair with the heat setting too high the pre-glued tips start to melt and get gooey.. even with the beads on. . After I applied some extensions in my hair I was excited I had hair that looked balanced and my thinning spots ( on sides of my head) looked fuller. It was great until I washed my hair. I followed the directions and was very careful combing them out. They started to frizz up and shed a little after my second wash. I was somewhat disappointed at that point. I hadn’t had that happen before. I could have made a little birds nest after a month. I did get my grow out (roots) colored and the color looked great on the extensions. I wish the hair pieces were a little fuller and the advertised length was really the true length. The issues I experienced is not with the seller but the manufacturer for not disclosing the fact it is synthetic hair. The price I paid is much less than others I noticed. You get what you pay for is my opinion.

text:
Doesn't matter where you buy them, who you buy from, etc. There is a 50% chance you get a good bottle of polish and a 50% chance you get a bad one. I have 3 bottles from 3 different places, 3 different colors (blue white and black) and the blue one is good, the white one is meh, and the black one is completely useless. The white and black have bent brushes, and the white polish is a bit dried out but not the worst, kind of reminds me of using white-out before they changed the formula and it got all watery and they changed to a sponge applicator from a brush. The black one is awful - it's literally just clumps of dried out polish with some occasional damp spots. The blue one is okay, the polish is alright, but I wouldn't recommend using the brushes that came with these but buying a separate skinny brush for line work or whatever it is you want to do.

I should mention I have been using them to fix an enameled piece of jewelry as opposed to doing nails, as that's what the jeweler recommended (nobody local repairs enamel resin and buying enamel is very expensive and you end up with a ton leftover), so a jeweler I trust recommended nail polish with a good resin sealant and that's what I've been doing. Initially I had used an X-Acto knife to cut painter's tape around the shapes of different colors and metal but switched to this pink-colored liquid latex paint I saw on a youtube video for nail designs and it's much easier.

text:
This DOES NOT stain my tub. This DOES NOT stain my skin. This DOES NOT smell like chemicals or burning hair. This DOES work better than "Color Safe" or "Sulfate Free" shampoos that I've tried.

I bought this at Sally's and I love it. I now use it daily/every other day. I have copper/auburn hair with dark mahogany tones and this really helps keep the reds vivid.

I read all the reviews about this and decided to try it even though it's not sulfate free. I was afraid that because this product isn't sulfate free that it would strip out the color deposits and be redundant, but it really doesn't do that... It doesn't make my hair any softer or more brittle than other shampoos, it just leaves my hair feeling clean and more vivid. I've already seen results after my first 2 uses. It also smells really nice, not like chemicals or anything like the other negative reviews had said.

Obviously this isn't going to work with fire engine red hair, that dye is bound to fade even if you look at it the wrong way (I know from experience). I'd recommend this to anyone with almost any shade of red colored hair who just wants to keep their color more vibrant in between dyes. Have realistic expectations, and you'll be pleasantly surprised =)

text:
This is a great hair styling tool. My hair is very curly, and slightly on the coarse side. At first I was concerned that this could style or straighten my hair. So this brush is actually easy to use. Think of it as a heated paddle brush. First thing I noticed was the quality construction of the brush. Brush it's self is not heavy, but feels very comfortable in your hand. Used this brush in several ways, and way I had best success was first spraying my hair with a thermal protecting spray. Next make sure to brush or come my hair to make sure it's free of tangles. My hair is very curly, and often uncontrollable. Took small sections of my hair, and held my hair taught, and went down the length of my hair with the brush. First pass my hair became a loose curl. After the second pass my hair was straight. Love the bright temperature display on the handle. Thankfully the power, + & - buttons are high up enough your hand wont switch them. Oh there is also a lock setting which I like. Also tested this hair on my cousins wavy hair, and her hair straightened on the first pass. Noticed my hair has a nice shine to it after using this brush. It's very easy to use, and my brothers also like using it. A great product.

text: Disappointed. The scents include a "musk", florals (Jasmine, Lily, Honeysuckle) and a "vanilla", which should be a warning. High-end designer scents may be derived or contain some of these "notes", but do not stop there. The "musk" referred to as "amber musk" is the most subtle and acceptable of these; it is somewhat "softer" than the old, familiar drugstore variety that had its heyday in the '80s. The florals smell cheap; very few fragrances use essence of living flowers and that is necessary to distill a true floral scent. The jasmine has a "brassy" top note as does the honeysuckle. No one should do "vanilla" anymore; it is a cloying and overwhelming smell (not scent), rather than a complex "caramel", and this one is no different. (Acqua di Parma is the only line that includes "vanilla" notes in its scents that have class and understatement.) These scents are nicely packaged in glass bottles and a laminated cardboard box with the brand name stenciled on it. But not nearly worth the price.

text:
I am a big fan of nail wraps because of all the different designs and how easy it is to do your nails. However, I was disappointed with these ones on the 3rd day.

Application was pretty straight forward like many others. Comes with a small nail file and alcohol pad to remove all the oil from your nails. I had to use my own wood stick to push the cuticles back. They looked great on day 1 and 2, however, after that, 2 nails started chipping and one (the ring finger) had a hole on it! Now idea how that happened, I'm not doing anything rough/tough with my hands!

Anyway, they do not peel off however they seem to be very thin that are not durable at all. There is no way these wraps will last 14 days as advertised.

Also, on the instructions, they did not recommend applying a top coat once done, since the supposed to have that top coat already. But it might be worth adding a top coat layer on your own to make them last a couple of extra days.

text:
Bought this based on reviews/reputation as a travel soap (stick is easy to cary around when i travel, my Tabac puck stays in it's cup at home.) Performance-wise it's brilliant. Richer lather than Tabac and very smooth. The stick will last you a really long time. For the price it's a fantastic bargain.

My one complaint, and it's a very subjective one, is that I can't stand the scent. It has a very perfume-y odor. It almost makes my eyes water. I tried to get used to it. I couldn't. I know a lot of people actually like the scent, it's just not for me. I tend to like more natural, subtle scents. The Arko smells artificial and overpowering to me. I can smell it from 30 feet away when it's sitting out- that's how over-the-top I find this soap.

Like I said, this is subjective. If you can deal with the scent, it's a great product.

text:
I was excited to get this Jade Roller Bottle, and was even happier when it arrived! It's a larger size than I thought it was going to be -- larger than a lip gloss roller bottle, maybe 2.5 times the size in diameter, but still a good size for facial massage.

The pink jade roller is very smooth (sometimes they are not), and rolls very easily and doesn't get stuck. But, it doesn't roll as smoothly as say, a stainless steel roller ball. There will be a catch every now and then, but I think that's what you can expect with a natural stone. To me, it's worth it because you get the coolness of the jade, and I suppose some healing qualities, but I'm not sure what the science is on that. But, it's nice and relaxing to use as part of your bedtime routine with some nice oil to moisturize and massage your face.

text: If standing on one foot on a wet, soapy shower floor isn't an issue for you, you don't need this.  For those who have balance and mobility trouble, it's a safer way to scrub and exfoliate your feet and clean between your toes.  It's well made and with daily use hasn't shown any signs of falling apart.  The bristles are fairly soft but diabetics and anyone else who must guard against cuts on the feet should be cautious during use.  The attached pumice stone is positioned to work on the heels, but just place the ball of your foot on the stone without putting your whole foot inside if you need to exfoliate that area as well.  UPDATE: After more use I agree with warnings that the suction cups DO NOT hold it in place.  If standing on one foot is a problem, use this only while seated.  If standing, keep your free foot planted firmly and never put your weight on the foot inside the scrubber. It will be very uncomfortable and will probably slip out from under you.

text:
Born Pretty is one of my favorite nail polish brands. I've been buying their products for a while and I'm really excited that they're available with Prime! The magnetic nail polish set is a great deal.

For first time uses of polish like this there are a couple of things that you need to do in order for it to turn out like the pictures. You must use a black base coat as well as buying a separate magnetic stick. It won't turn out like the picture unless you have those two additional items. There are a couple of great tutorials on Youtube that you can watch to achieve the look.

Born Pretty nail polishes dry fast and do not have a strong smell. Born Pretty's nail polishes have barely any scent, next to zero. I compare Born Pretty nail polishes to my numerous American and European brands I use (Essie, Sally Hansen, OPI, Kiko Milano, Sinful Colors and ect.) and Born Pretty nail polishes always dry down fast and are basically scentless. I also have sensitive skin and have never had any problems.

One of the best brands I've ever tried and will continue to do so!
